item.title: Upadacitinib-induced interstitial pneumonitis.
item.description: BMJ case reports - Case report - Drug-induced lung injury represents a heterogeneous group of adverse reactions, with an increasing number of implicated medications each year. We report a case of pneumonitis in a woman recently co...

item.title: Upadacitinib as a rescue therapy for steroid- and infliximab-refractory acute severe ulcerative colitis: short- and long-term effectiveness and safety in a multicenter cohort study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Acute severe active ulcerative colitis (ASUC) often requires rescue therapy when standard medical treatment fails. Data on the use of upadacitinib (UPA) as a rescue option after failure of steroids...

item.title: Subcutaneous infliximab in pediatric inflammatory bowel disease: Sustained remission, predictable pharmacokinetics, and real-world US implementation.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - Subcutaneous infliximab (IFX-SC) offers a convenient alternative to intravenous infliximab (IFX-IV) in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) with more stable concentrations. Pediatric experience remains...

item.title: Ocrelizumab-induced colitis acutely managed with ciclosporin.
item.description: BMJ case reports - Case report - Ocrelizumab is an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody widely used in the treatment of multiple sclerosis (MS). Although rare, ocrelizumab-induced colitis is a recognised and potentially severe condition ...

item.title: Subcutaneous infliximab in the treatment of patients with perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease: current evidence and emerging roles.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - Review - Perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease (pfCD), a frequent and serious manifestation of Crohn's disease (CD), is characterized by perianal inflammation and fistula formation and severely impacts patie...

item.title: Corticosteroid therapy following infliximab rescue in acute severe ulcerative colitis: should they be continued?
item.description: Internal medicine journal - In patients with acute severe ulcerative colitis (ASUC) who have received infliximab rescue therapy, the need for and timing of further steroid therapy is unclear. This brief communication explores...

item.title: Meta-analysis: safety and efficacy of ustekinumab in pediatric inflammatory bowel disease patients with anti-TNF failure.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Systematic review - Evidence for ustekinumab (UST) in pediatric inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is limited, particularly in patients who do not respond to anti-tumor necrosis factor (anti-TNF) therapy. We conducted a...

item.title: Effectiveness of tofacitinib as rescue therapy in biological-naive patients with steroid-refractory acute severe ulcerative colitis: a multicentric retrospective analysis.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - Tofacitinib, a Janus kinase inhibitor approved for moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis, has limited data in acute severe ulcerative colitis (ASUC), particularly in biologic-naive patients. This s...

item.title: Study of TOfacitinib for the treatment of chronic PouchITis (STOPit): a multi-centre open label induction with randomised, double blind, placebo-controlled maintenance trial.
item.description: BMJ open - RCT - Study of TOfacitinib for the treatment of chronic PouchITis (STOPit) is a national, investigator-led, multicentre, open-label induction with randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled maintenance...

item.title: Subcutaneous infliximab as a maintenance option in pediatric IBD: a real-world cohort including younger and lower-weight children.
item.description: Molecular and cellular pediatrics - Subcutaneous infliximab (SC-IFX) offers an alternative to intravenous infliximab (IV-IFX) in adults with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), but evidence in children is scarce. This study reports tre...

item.title: The dual role of RIPK family in IBD: from cell death to therapeutic targeting.
item.description: Molecular medicine (Cambridge, Mass.) - Review - The receptor-interacting serine/threonine-protein kinase (RIPK) family, particularly RIPK1, RIPK2, and RIPK3, functions as a central regulatory hub at the intersection of cell death and inflammator...

item.title: Hepatic and splenic tuberculosis following infliximab therapy for refractory ulcerative colitis: a case report and literature review.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Tumor necrosis factor-α (TNF-α) inhibitors are first-line agents for steroid-refractory/resistant ulcerative colitis (UC), but their immunosuppressive effect elevates the risk of opportunistic infe...

item.title: Reactive Therapeutic Drug Monitoring Guides Optimal Management of Adalimumab Failure in Pediatric Crohn's Disease: A Real-World Single-Center Experience.
item.description: Pediatric gastroenterology, hepatology & nutrition - Although biologics, including anti-tumor necrosis factor (TNF) drugs, are increasingly used for treating pediatric Crohn's disease (CD), the potential loss of response (LOR) to these drugs requires...

item.title: Engineered Escherichia coli Nissle 1917 secreting anti-TNF-α nanobody as a single-strain live biotherapeutic for inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - The rising global incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) creates an urgent need for safer, gut-targeted therapies. Current treatments, from small-molecule drugs to systemic anti-tumor necros...
